headline: CFTC Suspends 2 Officials Over Crypto Market Dispute

article.title: CFTC officials who questioned prediction markets were suspended: NYT
article.summary: A New York Times investigation found that senior CFTC officials who raised concerns about Polymarket, Crypto.com and Gemini were suspended and pushed out.

headline: Ethereum Foundation Passes $1Bn Test Amid Criticism

article.title: Blockchain researcher defends Ethereum Foundation, says it’s ‘exactly’ doing its job
article.summary: William Mougayar says critics are measuring the Ethereum Foundation by the wrong standard, claiming it was never meant to pump ETH or court institutions.

headline: Emerging Markets Plunge 3% on Rising Political Risk

article.title: What’s Happening in EM: Political Risk Roils Markets (Podcast)
article.summary: Maria Elena Vizcaino and Zijia Song discuss the fresh wave of political risk rippling across emerging markets, how they’re affecting financial assets and how investors are positioning for the resurgent jitters.

headline: PCE Inflation Surges 6.1% Amid War Fears

article.title: More War-Driven Inflation Seen in Fed’s Favored Gauge
article.summary: The Federal Reserve’s favored top-line inflation gauge is rapidly approaching 4% as a war-driven spike in energy costs generates unease that price pressures will broaden.

article.title: Bitcoin Dives Below $75K for First Time in a Month as Crypto Liquidations Near $1 Billion
article.summary: Bitcoin touched its lowest price in a month overnight following an awful week for ETFs, which shed over $1.25 billion this week.

headline: ECB Warns EU: Easing Stablecoin Rules Hits Banks

article.title: ECB warns EU finance ministers that easing euro stablecoin rules would weaken banks: Reuters
article.summary: ECB President Christine Lagarde warned the changes would destabilize bank funding and weaken interest-rate transmission.

headline: FTX Partners Pay $66M in Crypto Fraud Settlement

article.title: FTX’s former law firm, auditor agree to pay $66 million to settle customer claims over fraud
article.summary: The law firm, Fenwick & West, denied wrongdoing and still faces a separate $525 million suit in Washington that is not covered by Friday's deal.
